purpose: Classify Phase 9 feat implementation families by current runtime fit: exists wholecloth, exists partially and needs adapters, exists partially and needs refactor, or does not exist yet.

classificationDefinitions.exists_wholecloth: Runtime home already exists and is appropriate; remaining work is mostly metadata normalization/tests for individual feats.
classificationDefinitions.exists_partial_needs_adapters: A reusable runtime home exists, but Phase 9 feats need thin adapters, registry entries, event bridges, or metadata-to-runtime mapping.
classificationDefinitions.exists_partial_needs_refactor: Some supporting runtime exists, but the available shape is too shallow or bypasses the right lifecycle; implement by refactoring around the existing authority, not by adding a parallel engine.
classificationDefinitions.missing_runtime: No meaningful runtime home was found; implementation requires a new authority/module, still routed through existing mutation and chat boundaries.

rules: Use every part of the buffalo: prefer existing engines, registries, and mutation authorities over parallel systems.
Feat metadata is not implementation proof unless a runtime consumer reads and enforces it.
Wrong-shape automation remains implemented_incorrect until replaced or quarantined.
New runtime adapters must route actor mutation through ActorEngine and resource limits through ActivationLimitEngine or existing resource services.

families.evidence: CombatOptionResolver already discovers ATTACK_OPTION rules, hydrates toggles/sliders/passive options, validates weapon/context gates, and contributes attack, damage, extra weapon dice, defense modifiers, critical threat, and target effect data.
families.nextStep: Normalize remaining attack-option feats into abilityMeta.rules and add targeted tests for option gating, sliders, and breakdown parity.

families.evidence: The resolver consumes WEAPON_DAMAGE_DIE_STEP, UNARMED_DAMAGE_STEP, ATTACK_ABILITY_SUBSTITUTION, ATTACK_ABILITY_BONUS, WEAPON_ATTACK_BONUS, WEAPON_DAMAGE_BONUS, EXTEND_CRITICAL_RANGE, CRITICAL_RIDER, HIT_RIDER, and WEAPON_CRITICAL_MULTIPLIER_MIN.
families.nextStep: Prefer this existing rule vocabulary for weapon-bound feats before creating any new feat-specific runtime.

families.evidence: ImplantRules detects active implants and Implant Training, suppresses Will and condition-track drawbacks, and is consumed by defense and condition/damage flows.
families.nextStep: Keep Phase 10A override: Implant Training is implemented_correct in the current repo state. Add regression tests rather than a new subsystem.

families.evidence: ReactionEngine and ReactionRegistry exist and resolve registered reactions, but many Phase 9 feat reactions are not yet registered or connected to the exact combat event that triggers them.
families.nextStep: Add feat-specific reaction registry entries and small event adapters. Do not create a second reaction engine.

families.evidence: ActivationLimitEngine can gate round/encounter/day-like use, and AbilityExecutionRouter is the intended activation entry point, but many feat actions still need adapters to route through it.
families.nextStep: Bind feat action cards/buttons to AbilityExecutionRouter or EncounterUseTracker consistently; keep DAY persistence caveat visible.

families.evidence: DamageResolutionEngine already centralizes pure damage, mitigation, threshold, and condition-track outcomes, and MetaResourceFeatResolver already feeds some damage/condition rules. Missing pieces are pre/post-hit adapters for attacker-side timing/rider feats.
families.nextStep: Add attacker/target result hooks around attack success and damage resolution; keep Advantageous Attack implemented_incorrect until its wrong-shape attack option is replaced by this hook.

families.evidence: Force point spend/max/die-size and some reroll/recovery metadata bridges exist, but out-of-turn/free-action permission and several spend-result riders need explicit action/event adapters.
families.nextStep: Implement Force Readiness as a Force Point spend permission adapter, not as a Force feat bonus. Continue using ActorEngine/ForcePointsService for mutation.

families.evidence: ForceEngine currently covers DC, Force Point spend, natural 20 recovery, dark side points, descriptor modifiers, and cost checks, while AbilityExecutionRouter delegates force-power activations. It does not yet expose a rich named-power activation/result context for scoped bonuses.
families.nextStep: Refactor force-power activation through a named power context that can consume scoped feat metadata before/after rolls; avoid hardcoding each Forceful feat.

families.evidence: MetaResourceFeatResolver can read second-wind rule families including free action, condition recovery, Force power recovery, movement grants, extra healing, and delayed healing. Some effects still need UI choice/pending-state adapters.
families.nextStep: Wire pending choice flows for recovered Force powers and movement grants into existing second-wind UX.

families.evidence: Skill derivation and progression exist, and attack reroll/temporary defense feat bridges exist, but there is no proven generic skill-use action modal that owns action cost, Take 10/Take 20, rushed checks, time reduction, or failed-skill rerolls.
families.nextStep: Create a skill-use runtime adapter/modal that consumes feat metadata and uses ActivationLimitEngine for encounter limits. Do not overload derived skill totals.

families.evidence: The attack option resolver has area/autofire/weapon gates and damage resolution can resolve mitigated damage, but grid template shape, cover provider identity, soft cover miss redirection, and multi-target area outcomes are not unified into a single context object yet.
families.nextStep: Refactor attack result context for area/template attacks so cover, soft-cover providers, ally bonuses, and per-target riders can attach safely.

families.evidence: No dedicated poison/disease/hazard condition authority was identified in this pass. Current metadata can describe conditional defense, but there is no proven PoisonEngine-style hook for Fortitude targeting, half damage, delayed saves, or ongoing track effects.
families.nextStep: Introduce a small PoisonDiseaseHazardRules authority that feeds defense context and damage-resolution context, with ActorEngine as the mutation boundary.

families.evidence: Some reactions can report movement permissions, but there is no general movement/action authority that validates threatened squares, Withdraw, Tumble DC changes, or forced movement against the canvas/grid.
families.nextStep: Add movement-control authority only when canvas/action enforcement is ready; until then keep these feats partial/manual.

families.evidence: There are resource and activation primitives, but no proven Aid Another support workflow that owns helper, target, recipient, success, stacking, range, and expiration.
families.nextStep: Implement a small AidAnotherSupportAdapter instead of adding flat ally bonuses to sheets.

families.evidence: No dedicated rage-state authority was identified. Rage feats should not become always-on Strength, skill, or action modifiers.
families.nextStep: Create a RageStateRules adapter only after defining the rage lifecycle/state flag, skill permission behavior, and duration handling.
